fault_hypothesis: The rate limit check on line 70 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), which incorrectly allows a new request when the number of recent pings is exactly equal to the limit. It should use a greater-than-or-equal-to comparison (`>=`) to deny requests when the limit has been reached.

constraints.intent: The decision logic is flawed for the boundary case where the number of recent pings equals the limit.

spec.expr: (__cbfl_result[0] and len(recent) < limit) or (not __cbfl_result[0] and len(recent) >= limit)

constraints.intent: A request should be allowed if and only if the number of recent pings is strictly less than the limit.
If the request is allowed (i.e., this branch is taken), the number of recent pings must be strictly less than the limit.
The logic fails when the number of recent pings exactly matches the limit, which should be a handled case.
